title: Spartium cupanii
taxonomy: Spartium cupanii is a species of legume, belonging to the genus Spartium and the family Fabaceae. The genus Spartium is a part of the subfamily Faboideae. intro: Spartium cupanii, also known as Cupani's broom or Spanish broom, is a flowering shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects that can help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, wounds, and infections, as well as support cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Spartium cupanii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat skin conditions and as a diuretic.
